content.problemStatement:
You have NNN tasks, indexed 111 to NNN, where the iii-th of which takes AiA_iAi​ time to be completed by a skilled worker, and can be started only after the time SiS_iSi​ (including exactly at SiS_iSi​).

You have MMM workers, indexed 111 to MMM, where the iii-th of which has a skill deficiency factor of FiF_iFi​ meaning that if a skilled worker would take ttt time to complete the task, this worker will take Fi⋅tF_i \cdot tFi​⋅t time.

Suppose PiP_iPi​ is the worker assigned to task iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N). We require that PPP is a non-decreasing sequence, i.e. Pi≥Pi−1P_i \ge P_{i - 1}Pi​≥Pi−1​.

Find the earliest possible time where we can complete all the required tasks.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains NNN and MMM - the number of tasks and the number of workers
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,....,ANA_1, A_2, ...., A_NA1​,A2​,....,AN​ : time required for a skilled worker to complete the iii-th task.
The third line contains NNN integers - S1,S2,....,SNS_1, S_2, ...., S_NS1​,S2​,....,SN​ : minimum possible starting time for the iii-th task.
The fourth line contains MMM integers - F1,F2,...,FMF_1, F_2, ..., F_MF1​,F2​,...,FM​ : skill deficiency factor of the iii-th worker.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum possible time when all tasks are completed.
constraints: The sum of NNN and the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 2 : Assign the first 222 tasks to the first worker, and the 333rd task to the second worker. Then, P=[1,1,2]P = [1, 1, 2]P=[1,1,2] is the array formed. This is non decreasing as required in the statement.
The worker 111 first works on the first task, starting at 111, finishing it by time 333. His skill deficiency factor was 222 hence he took 222 time units to complete the task. Then, he instantly moves on to the 222nd task, starting it immediately, and finishing it by time 999.
The worker 222 starts at time 333, and takes 999 units because his skill deficiency is 333 and the normal task time is 333. Thus, both workers finish their work by 121212 and this is optimal.
Test Case 3 : All tasks are assigned to worker 111.
